title: Spacing in Foundational hand — between letters and words
chartDefinition: calligraphyExemplar — Foundational lowercase, showing the three letter-spacing groups: two curved strokes (oo), curved + straight (no), two straight strokes (nn)
sources: Edward Johnston, Writing and Illuminating and Lettering (1906) — chapters on spacing principles and even colour
notes: Johnston's counter-shape equality rule: the white space between letters should be optically equal to the white inside the letter counters. The three spacing groups: two rounds (oo), round + arch (no), two arches (nn) — each pair sets the tone. Word spacing = a condensed letter-o. Practical exercises in spotting and correcting uneven colour.
